speaker: A
english: Hello, may I see your passport please?

speaker: B
english: Sure, here you are.

speaker: A
english: What is the purpose of your visit?

speaker: B
english: I'm here for sightseeing.

speaker: A
english: How long will you be staying?

speaker: B
english: I'll stay for two weeks.

speaker: A
english: Do you have anything to declare?

speaker: B
english: No, nothing to declare.

speaker: A
english: Where will you be staying?

speaker: B
english: I have a hotel reservation downtown.

speaker: A
english: Could you place your bag on the belt?

speaker: B
english: Sure, no problem.

speaker: A
english: Please remove your laptop from the bag.

speaker: B
english: Okay, here it is.

speaker: A
english: Your boarding gate is number twelve.

speaker: B
english: Thank you. When does boarding start?

speaker: A
english: Boarding begins at three thirty.

speaker: B
english: Where is the lounge?

speaker: A
english: Go straight and turn left at the end of the hall.

speaker: B
english: Got it. Thank you for your help.

speaker: A
english: Welcome! How many in your party?

speaker: B
english: A table for two, please.

speaker: A
english: Right this way. Here are your menus.

speaker: B
english: Thank you. Could we have some water?

speaker: A
english: Of course. Are you ready to order?

speaker: B
english: I'd like the steak, medium rare.

speaker: A
english: Would you like soup or salad with that?
korean: 수프와 샐러드 중 어떤 걸로 드릴까요?

speaker: B
english: I'll have the salad, please.

speaker: A
english: Anything to drink?

speaker: B
english: A glass of red wine would be great.

speaker: A
english: Here's your steak. Enjoy your meal.

speaker: B
english: This looks delicious, thank you.

speaker: A
english: Is everything okay with your meal?

speaker: B
english: Yes, it's wonderful.

speaker: A
english: Would you like to see the dessert menu?

speaker: B
english: Yes, please.

speaker: A
english: I recommend the chocolate cake.

speaker: B
english: I'll try that. And could we get the bill?

speaker: A
english: Of course. Will it be cash or card?

speaker: B
english: Card, please. Keep the change.

speaker: A
english: Good evening, welcome to our hotel.
korean: 안녕하세요, 저희 호텔에 오신 것을 환영합니다.

speaker: B
english: Hi, I have a reservation under Kim.

speaker: A
english: Let me check. May I see your ID?

speaker: B
english: Sure, here is my passport.

speaker: A
english: You're booked for three nights, correct?

speaker: B
english: Yes, that's right.

speaker: A
english: Would you prefer a city or ocean view?
korean: 시티뷰와 오션뷰 중 어떤 걸로 드릴까요?

speaker: B
english: Ocean view, please, if possible.

speaker: A
english: Your room number is 1203.

speaker: B
english: What time is breakfast served?

speaker: A
english: From six thirty to ten in the morning.

speaker: B
english: Where is the dining room?

speaker: A
english: It's on the second floor.

speaker: B
english: Is there free Wi-Fi in the rooms?

speaker: A
english: Yes, the password is on the desk in your room.
korean: 네, 비밀번호는 객실 책상 위에 있습니다.

speaker: B
english: Could I get a wake-up call at seven?

speaker: A
english: Certainly, I've noted that down.

speaker: B
english: One more thing — can I store my luggage?
korean: 한 가지 더요. 짐을 보관할 수 있을까요?

speaker: A
english: Yes, just leave it with the front desk.

speaker: B
english: Perfect. Thank you so much.

speaker: A
english: How can I help you today?

speaker: B
english: I have a really bad headache.

speaker: A
english: How long has this been going on?

speaker: B
english: Since yesterday morning.

speaker: A
english: Do you have any other symptoms?

speaker: B
english: I feel a little dizzy as well.

speaker: A
english: Have you taken any medication?

speaker: B
english: I took some painkillers but they didn't help.

speaker: A
english: Let me check your blood pressure.

speaker: B
english: Is something wrong?

speaker: A
english: Your blood pressure is a bit high.

speaker: B
english: Should I be worried?

speaker: A
english: I'll prescribe some medicine. Please rest.

speaker: B
english: How often should I take the medicine?

speaker: A
english: Three times a day after meals.

speaker: B
english: Are there any side effects?

speaker: A
english: You may feel a little drowsy.

speaker: B
english: Should I come back if it gets worse?
korean: 더 안 좋아지면 다시 와야 하나요?

speaker: A
english: Yes, come back in three days.

speaker: B
english: Thank you, doctor.

speaker: A
english: Hi, can I help you find something?

speaker: B
english: I'm looking for a winter jacket.

speaker: A
english: What size are you?

speaker: B
english: I usually wear medium.

speaker: A
english: This one is very popular.

speaker: B
english: Can I try it on?

speaker: A
english: Sure, the fitting room is over there.

speaker: B
english: It fits perfectly. How much is it?

speaker: A
english: It's on sale for ninety dollars.

speaker: B
english: Do you have it in black?

speaker: A
english: Let me check the back. One moment.

speaker: B
english: Take your time.

speaker: A
english: Yes, here is the black one.

speaker: B
english: Great, I'll take it.

speaker: A
english: Will that be cash or card?

speaker: B
english: Card. Do you have a return policy?

speaker: A
english: You can return within thirty days with the receipt.

speaker: B
english: Could I get a bag, please?

speaker: A
english: Of course. Here's your receipt.

speaker: B
english: Thanks, have a great day.

speaker: A
english: Excuse me, could you help me?

speaker: B
english: Of course, what do you need?

speaker: A
english: How do I get to the museum?

speaker: B
english: It's about a ten-minute walk.

speaker: A
english: Could you show me on the map?

speaker: B
english: Sure. We are right here.

speaker: A
english: Should I take the subway?

speaker: B
english: Walking is faster from here.

speaker: A
english: Which direction should I go?

speaker: B
english: Go straight for two blocks.

speaker: A
english: Then what?

speaker: B
english: Turn left at the bank.

speaker: A
english: Is there a landmark I should look for?

speaker: B
english: There's a big fountain in front of it.

speaker: A
english: I see. Is it open today?

speaker: B
english: Yes, but it closes at six.

speaker: A
english: That's plenty of time. Thank you.

speaker: B
english: No problem. Enjoy your visit.

speaker: A
english: One more thing — where can I get coffee?
korean: 한 가지만 더요. 커피는 어디서 사나요?

speaker: B
english: There's a nice cafe right next door.

speaker: A
english: Hi, what can I get for you?

speaker: B
english: One iced americano, please.

speaker: A
english: What size would you like?

speaker: B
english: A medium, please.

speaker: A
english: Anything else?

speaker: B
english: Could I get a chocolate croissant?
korean: 초콜릿 크루아상 하나 주실 수 있을까요?

speaker: A
english: Sure. For here or to go?

speaker: B
english: For here, please.

speaker: A
english: Can I get your name for the order?

speaker: B
english: It's Min.

speaker: A
english: That'll be six dollars total.

speaker: B
english: Here's my card.

speaker: A
english: Would you like a receipt?

speaker: B
english: Yes, please.

speaker: A
english: Your drink will be ready shortly.

speaker: B
english: Where can I find napkins?

speaker: A
english: Right by the counter.

speaker: B
english: Is the Wi-Fi free?

speaker: A
english: Yes, the password is on your receipt.

speaker: B
english: Thanks. Have a good day.

speaker: A
english: Hi, I don't think we've met. I'm Mark.
korean: 안녕하세요, 처음 뵙는 것 같네요. 저는 마크예요.

speaker: B
english: Nice to meet you, Mark. I'm Jisoo.

speaker: A
english: Where are you from?

speaker: B
english: I'm from Seoul, Korea.

speaker: A
english: Oh, I've always wanted to visit.

speaker: B
english: You should! It's a great city.

speaker: A
english: What do you do for a living?

speaker: B
english: I work in software development.

speaker: A
english: That sounds interesting.

speaker: B
english: How about you?

speaker: A
english: I'm a high school teacher.

speaker: B
english: That must be rewarding.

speaker: A
english: It is, most days.

speaker: B
english: How long have you lived here?

speaker: A
english: About five years now.

speaker: B
english: Do you have any hobbies?

speaker: A
english: I love hiking on weekends.

speaker: B
english: That's awesome. Me too.

speaker: A
english: We should go sometime.

speaker: B
english: I'd love that. Here's my number.

speaker: A
english: Thank you for coming today.

speaker: B
english: My pleasure. Thanks for having me.

speaker: A
english: Shall we start with introductions?

speaker: B
english: Sure. I'm the project manager from Seoul.
korean: 네. 저는 서울에서 온 프로젝트 매니저입니다.

speaker: A
english: Let me share our quarterly results.

speaker: B
english: Could you walk us through the highlights?

speaker: A
english: Revenue grew by fifteen percent.

speaker: B
english: That's impressive. What drove the growth?

speaker: A
english: Mostly our new product line.

speaker: B
english: How is the market reacting?

speaker: A
english: Customer feedback has been positive.

speaker: B
english: What about next quarter's targets?

speaker: A
english: We aim for twenty percent growth.

speaker: B
english: That sounds ambitious.

speaker: A
english: We have a clear strategy in place.

speaker: B
english: Could we discuss the budget?

speaker: A
english: Let me bring up the slides.

speaker: B
english: I have some questions about the timeline.
korean: 일정에 대해 몇 가지 질문이 있습니다.

speaker: A
english: Please go ahead.

speaker: B
english: Let's schedule a follow-up next week.

speaker: A
english: Good morning, ABC Company. How can I help you?

speaker: B
english: Hi, this is John. May I speak to Susan?
korean: 안녕하세요, 존입니다. 수잔과 통화할 수 있을까요?

speaker: A
english: I'm sorry, she's in a meeting right now.

speaker: B
english: When will she be available?

speaker: A
english: She should be free after three.

speaker: B
english: Could you take a message?

speaker: A
english: Of course. What's your message?

speaker: B
english: Please ask her to call me back.

speaker: A
english: May I have your phone number?

speaker: A
english: Could you repeat that, please?

speaker: A
english: And what's this regarding?

speaker: B
english: It's about our project deadline.

speaker: A
english: I'll let her know right away.

speaker: B
english: Is there a better time to reach her?

speaker: A
english: Mornings are usually best.

speaker: B
english: I'll try again tomorrow morning.

speaker: A
english: Thank you for calling.

speaker: B
english: Thanks for your help. Goodbye.

speaker: A
english: Please have a seat. Thanks for coming.

speaker: B
english: Thank you for the opportunity.

speaker: A
english: Could you tell me about yourself?

speaker: B
english: I have five years of experience in marketing.

speaker: A
english: Why are you interested in this role?
korean: 이 직무에 관심을 가지신 이유는 무엇인가요?

speaker: B
english: Your company's vision really aligns with mine.
korean: 귀사의 비전이 제 가치관과 잘 맞아서요.

speaker: A
english: What are your strengths?

speaker: B
english: I'm a strong communicator and team player.

speaker: A
english: And your weaknesses?

speaker: B
english: I sometimes focus too much on details.
korean: 가끔 너무 세부 사항에 집중할 때가 있습니다.

speaker: A
english: How do you handle pressure?

speaker: B
english: I prioritize tasks and stay organized.

speaker: A
english: Tell me about a recent challenge.
korean: 최근에 겪은 도전에 대해 말씀해 주세요.

speaker: B
english: We launched a campaign with a tight deadline.
korean: 촉박한 일정으로 캠페인을 진행한 적이 있어요.

speaker: A
english: How did you handle it?

speaker: B
english: I delegated tasks and worked overtime.

speaker: A
english: Where do you see yourself in five years?
korean: 5년 후 본인의 모습은 어떨 것 같나요?

speaker: B
english: I hope to be in a leadership position.

speaker: A
english: Do you have any questions for us?

speaker: B
english: When can I expect to hear back?

speaker: A
english: Where to?

speaker: B
english: To Incheon Airport, please.

speaker: A
english: Which terminal?

speaker: B
english: Terminal two, please.

speaker: A
english: It will take about an hour.

speaker: B
english: Could you take the expressway?

speaker: A
english: Sure, but there's a toll.

speaker: B
english: That's fine. I'm in a hurry.

speaker: A
english: Please fasten your seatbelt.

speaker: B
english: Of course. Sorry, I forgot.

speaker: A
english: There's some traffic ahead.

speaker: B
english: Is there a faster route?

speaker: A
english: I can try the side road.

speaker: B
english: Please do whatever is fastest.

speaker: A
english: We are almost there.

speaker: B
english: How much do I owe you?

speaker: A
english: It's thirty-five dollars.

speaker: B
english: Could I get a receipt?

speaker: A
english: Sure, here you go.

speaker: B
english: Thank you for the safe ride.

speaker: A
english: Hi, how can I help you?

speaker: B
english: Two tickets for the eight o'clock show, please.

speaker: A
english: Which movie?

speaker: B
english: The new action film.

speaker: A
english: Where would you like to sit?

speaker: B
english: Somewhere in the middle.

speaker: A
english: How about row F, seats 10 and 11?

speaker: B
english: That works.

speaker: A
english: That'll be twenty-four dollars.

speaker: B
english: Do you have student discounts?

speaker: A
english: Yes, with a valid student ID.

speaker: B
english: Here you go.

speaker: A
english: Great, that'll be twenty dollars then.

speaker: B
english: Could I add some popcorn?

speaker: A
english: Sure. Small, medium, or large?
korean: 네. 스몰, 미디엄, 라지 중 어떤 걸로요?

speaker: B
english: Medium, with a soda.

speaker: A
english: That'll be eight dollars more.

speaker: B
english: When does the movie start?

speaker: A
english: In about fifteen minutes. Theater four is down the hall on your right.

speaker: B
english: Great, thanks!

speaker: A
english: Excuse me, I think I lost my wallet.

speaker: B
english: I'm sorry to hear that. Where did you last see it?

speaker: A
english: I had it at the cafe across the street.
korean: 길 건너 카페에 있을 때 가지고 있었어요.

speaker: B
english: Have you checked there yet?

speaker: A
english: Yes, but no luck.

speaker: B
english: You should report it to the police.

speaker: A
english: Where's the nearest police station?

speaker: B
english: Two blocks down on the left.

speaker: A
english: Thank you so much.

speaker: B
english: Did you have credit cards in it?

speaker: A
english: Yes, several.

speaker: B
english: You should call the bank to cancel them.

speaker: A
english: Good idea. I'll do that now.

speaker: B
english: Do you need to use my phone?

speaker: A
english: That would be a huge help.
korean: 그러면 정말 도움이 될 것 같아요.

speaker: B
english: Here you go. Take your time.

speaker: A
english: Hello, I need to report a lost card.

speaker: B
english: Are you okay otherwise?

speaker: A
english: Yes, just a bit shaken up.

speaker: B
english: Take a deep breath. Everything will be fine.

speaker: A
english: Welcome. How can I help you today?

speaker: B
english: Hi, I'd like to exchange some money.

speaker: A
english: Sure, what currencies?

speaker: B
english: From Korean won to US dollars.

speaker: A
english: How much would you like to exchange?

speaker: B
english: Five hundred thousand won.

speaker: A
english: Today's rate is 1,350 won per dollar.

speaker: B
english: Is there a service fee?

speaker: A
english: Yes, a small one.

speaker: B
english: That sounds fair.

speaker: A
english: Could I see your passport?

speaker: B
english: Here it is.

speaker: A
english: How would you like the bills?

speaker: B
english: Mostly twenties, please.

speaker: A
english: Sure. Anything else today?

speaker: B
english: I'd also like to open a savings account.

speaker: A
english: Please fill out this form.

speaker: B
english: Should I include my address?

speaker: A
english: Yes, your local address please.

speaker: B
english: All done. Thanks for your help.
